description: Master structural map of the 2026 Wellbeing Standard used by Beast System 3.0. Defines wellbeing domains, metrics, scoring logic, stabilization pathways, and trauma‑preventive governance rules.

housing_stability.purpose: Ensures safe, stable, non‑exploitative housing as a baseline human requirement.

food_and_nutrition.purpose: Ensures consistent access to nutritious food without stigma or deprivation.

health_and_mental_health.purpose: Ensures access to trauma‑informed, non‑punitive health and mental health support.

safety_and_non_violence.purpose: Ensures personal safety, freedom from violence, and protection from coercive systems.

dignity_and_rights.purpose: Ensures every person is treated with dignity, respect, and constitutional protections.

economic_stability.purpose: Ensures access to income, employment, and financial stability without exploitation.

community_and_spiritual_support.purpose: Ensures access to community, belonging, and spiritual grounding.

rules: no domain may be ignored due to institutional preference
scores must reflect lived experience, not assumptions

stabilization_engine.purpose: Defines stabilization pathways for individuals experiencing acute or chronic wellbeing deficits.

support_plan_engine.purpose: Defines individualized support plans based on assessment and stabilization outcomes.

follow_up_engine.purpose: Ensures continuity, accountability, and long‑term wellbeing stabilization.
rules: no case may be closed prematurely
support must continue until stability is achieved

harm_prevention_engine.purpose: Prevents harm, retraumatization, and institutional abuse.
rules: all harm events must be logged

federal_context.author: Pat Tarwater Jr.
federal_context.notes: The 2026 Wellbeing Standard is federally recognized as part of the case record and must remain immutable.
